title: Sticky Toffee Banana Bread

steps: Pre-heat your oven to 160 C / 140 C fan / Gas mark 3
Butter a 900g loaf tin and line with a strip of baking parchment paper.
Melting/boiling sugar is very very hot - be careful with all subsequent steps for making the toffee sauce
Beat 125 g, softened butter with both sugars ( 75 g, caster sugar & 50 g, dark brown soft sugar ) in a bowl with an electric whisk for 00:05:00 until very fluffy.
Whisk the 3 eggs, 2 mashed ripe bananas and 50g natural yoghurt together in a separate bowl until mostly smooth.
Whisk the egg/banana/yoghurt mix into the butter/sugar mix.
Add and fold in the 200 g, plain flour and 2 tsp baking powder to your mix until just combined.
Finally, add and fold in the 50 g, chopped dates and 50 g, chopped nuts .
Spoon into the tin, smooth the top, and bake for 01:00:00 - 01:15:00 until a skewer inserted into the middle comes out clean.
Leave to cool in the tin for 00:10:00 , then lift out using the strip of parchment.

You can serve warm now and eat if you want, or leave to cool completely on a wire rack. Banana break will keep for four days in an airtight container. This can also be frozen at this stage to use/eat at a later date.
Carefully whisk in the 25 g, butter until smooth.
Pour in the 100 mL, double cream slowly and bring to a bubble.
Mix until the sauce is smooth and shiny. Pour into a heatproof bowl, cover and leave to cool to Room temperature .

Put the 150 g, caster sugar in a frying pan over a medium heat with 2 tbsp water, stir briefly, then simmer for 00:10:00 until the mixture turns amber and makes caramel. Don't stir after the initial mix, just tilt the pan to get an even deep golden colour.
While waiting for the caramel, line a baking tray with baking parchment paper.

Carefully stir the nuts, banana chips and salt into the caramel to coat and then pour onto the lined baking tray. Tip the tray (or use a spoon) to make sure the nuts are in a single layer - they'll be harder to separate later.
Stir the toffee sauce to loosen, you may want to warm for a few seconds in the microwave (and you could try adding a drop more cream if needed).
Leave to cool completely and then break into shards.
Drizzle the toffee sauce over the cake so it drops down the sides.
Top with shards of brittle.
Slice & eat! Enjoy :) Your delicious sticky toffee banana bread will keep for two days in an airtight container.
Melt 100 g, light brown soft sugar in a heavy-based frying pan over a medium-low heat until light golden and liquid. Don't stir, just tilt the pan
Melting/boiling sugar is very very hot - be careful with all subsequent steps for making the caramel and brittle.
creator: Emma Ganley
